summary:
None of the retrieved documents provide information about G.V. Black’s instrument formula or the primary cutting edge angle in dental instruments. The reference to a 45-degree angle relates to ophthalmology, not dental restorations, thus invalidating any connection between the reported value and the query. 

\boxed{\text{C}} Despite insufficient evidence directly addressing G.V. Black’s formulation, the traditionally accepted cutoff below 50 degrees is selected under contextual alignment with common practice, even when not explicitly validated by retrieval outcomes.
